description_long: This object consists of a pair of Chinese dao swords, each featuring a single-edged, slightly curved blade with a dark metallic finish. The blades are long and taper to a sharp point, suitable for slashing and thrusting. The handles are wrapped in a brown material, likely wood or leather, and are fitted with a simple, functional crossguard and a curved pommel. The set includes matching sheaths, also brown, with visible fasteners and a subtle texture. The overall design is utilitarian with minimal ornamentation, emphasizing practicality and durability. The swords are well-crafted and appear robust, suitable for martial arts or display in an urban setting.
description: A pair of long, single-edged Chinese dao swords with dark blades, brown wrapped handles, and matching sheaths. The design is practical and robust, with minimal decoration.
description_view_0: Both swords are crossed, showing the full length of the blades and handles, with the sheaths visible and the crossguards clearly defined.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the curvature of the blades and the thickness of the sheaths, with the handles and pommels aligned vertically.
description_view_2: The blades are crossed, emphasizing the sharpness and length, with the handles and guards visible from a slightly angled perspective.
description_view_3: Top-down view reveals the slim profile of the blades and sheaths, with the handles and guards less prominent.
attribute_blade_type: single-edged, slightly curved, straight-backed dao blade
attribute_handle_design: straight, brown-wrapped, with a curved pommel and simple grip texture
